asymmetry: Your bankruptcy lawyer is legally allowed to send a random stranger to represent you in court for £60 while charging you thousands.
hook: Your lawyer isn't showing up. A stranger is.
core_fact: A website called MyMotionCalendar.com lets high-volume bankruptcy law firms hire random unqualified strangers for $75 to physically appear at your court hearing as a 'placeholder' — while you paid your actual attorney thousands of pounds for representation.
villain: High-volume bankruptcy law firms exploiting a legal loophole to ghost their own clients in court
share_trigger: Did you see this? Your solicitor can literally hire a random off the street to go to court instead of them and it's completely legal.
search_query: bankruptcy attorney sending stranger to court instead of showing up hearing substitute appearance lawyer
